title: Zeytoon Parvardeh (Marinated Olives)
description: A spectacular Northern Iranian appetizer consisting of green olives marinated in a rich, tangy paste of crushed walnuts, pomegranate molasses, garlic, and fresh herbs.

instructions.text: If your pitted green olives are very salty, soak them in cold water for an hour, then drain and pat dry. Place them in a large mixing bowl.
In a food processor, pulse the walnuts until they are finely ground, almost like a coarse meal. Do not turn them into butter.
Add the ground walnuts to the bowl with the olives.
Add the pomegranate molasses, minced garlic, chopped fresh mint, fresh cilantro, and olive oil to the bowl.
Toss everything together until the olives are thickly coated in the walnut-pomegranate mixture.
Transfer to a serving dish, cover, and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (preferably overnight) to allow the flavors to meld deeply.
Before serving, garnish with fresh pomegranate seeds.

tips: The authentic recipe uses Chochaq, a wild herb native to Northern Iran. A mix of mint and cilantro is the best substitute outside of Iran.
storage_notes: Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks. The flavor improves as it sits.
serve_with: Flatbreads, or as a side dish alongside fish and rice dishes like Sabzi Polo Mahi.

substitutions.note: Use 1 tablespoon dried mint if fresh herbs are unavailable.
In a pinch, use a thick balsamic glaze mixed with a little lemon juice, though pomegranate is highly preferred.
